target.title: fix #89668: [Bug]: WebChat can leak internal browser tool commentary/debug text into user-visible replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89668.

WebChat can leak internal browser tool commentary and debug text into user-visible assistant replies. The leak surfaces on two paths from the gateway to the WebChat client:

1. `chat.history` assistant text blocks still contain raw Harmony plain-text tool-call scaffolding such as `commentary to=functions.browser code {"action":"status"}` and the namespaced variant `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {...}`.
2. Live streaming `agent`/`chat` delta events broadcast the raw assistant text first and only sanitize afterwards, so a client that consumes the stream as it arrives still sees the scaffolding.

This change closes both paths by routing all assistant visible text through the existing `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` helper, which already understands the namespaced `functions.browser` / `functions.browser.commentary` grammar introduced earlier in this PR.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** the root cause is that the chat-display projection that feeds WebChat `chat.history` and the embedded-agent-subscribe non-phase streaming delta branch both emitted assistant text constructed from raw model output without routing it through the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` sanitizer, so raw Harmony plain-text tool-call scaffolding (`commentary to=functions.browser code {...}`, `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {...}`) reached the user verbatim on both the persisted history path and the live streaming path. The canonical sanitizer (the parser/normalizer in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`) already knew how to strip these forms because the underlying grammar mapping was extended earlier in this PR; the root cause was that two surfaces bypassed the contract and never invoked the canonical sanitizer, so the model-origin scaffolding leaked through unchanged.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the fix routes both surfaces through the same canonical sanitizer (`sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` for history, `sanitizeAssistantVisibleStreamText` for streaming) that already governs every other assistant-visible-text surface, instead of bolting on a one-off string filter at either surface. The source-of-truth contract is "assistant visible text is always sanitized through the canonical helper"; this PR makes the two surfaces that violated that contract comply with it, so the same fix automatically inherits any future extension to the sanitizer grammar (e.g., new scaffolding forms) via the upstream parser, without further per-surface patches. Because the canonical sanitizer is the root source of truth, fixing the contract violation here is the root-cause fix rather than a downstream patch.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`
  - Add `sanitizeAssistantChatHistoryText` and `truncateChatHistoryDisplayText` helpers.
  - Add `assistantVisibleText` option to `sanitizeChatHistoryContentBlock`; when set, assistant `text`/`content` is routed through `sanitizeAssistantChatHistoryText` instead of plain truncation.
  - Route assistant top-level `entry.content`, array content blocks (with `assistantVisibleText: role === "assistant"`), `entry.text`, and `projectAssistantTextFromMixedToolContent` text blocks through the sanitizer.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts`
  - In `handleMessageUpdate`'s recomputed branch and the non-phase `else` branch, run the assembled raw assistant text through `sanitizeAssistantVisibleStreamText` before computing `next` and `visibleDelta`, so streamed deltas never carry raw scaffolding.
- Tests
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`: regression covering `sanitizeChatHistoryMessages` stripping namespaced Harmony browser scaffolding from assistant history text blocks while preserving surrounding visible text.
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`: regression covering non-phase streaming deltas emitting sanitized text only.

## Review findings addressed

No actionable reviewer/bot/maintainer finding was present in the PR discussion. The linked issue (`#89668`) carries `clawsweeper:needs-live-repro`, which maps to a request for real behavior reproduction. The local gateway + WebChat RPC proof below addresses that request by reproducing the leak end-to-end against an isolated gateway built from this PR head and asserting both leak paths are closed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** WebChat must not surface raw Harmony plain-text tool-call scaffolding (`commentary to=functions.browser code {...}` and `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {...}`) to a user viewing assistant replies. The leak was observable through both persisted chat history (`chat.history`) and live streaming delta events (`agent` stream=assistant, `chat` message assistant). Linked issue #89668 reports exactly this leak; this PR closes both surfaces.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64. Isolated local OpenClaw gateway built from this PR head (`8d6efe75ace279d0383c549bf712ed3779b07a53` plus this commit) and spawned via `node scripts/run-with-env.mjs OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 -- node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway` with a throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, random auth token, loopback bind, and `dist/` rebuilt through `run-node.mjs`'s `dirty_watched_tree` path. A fake OpenAI-compatible HTTP provider (`/v1/chat/completions`, SSE streaming) bound to `127.0.0.1` returned the leak payload verbatim so the gateway truly ingested raw scaffolding. Client was Node.js 22 driving the same WebChat WebSocket RPC surface (`connect -> chat.send -> agent.wait -> chat.history`) that the browser Control UI uses, plus a passive listener recording every `agent`/`chat` event.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node proof.mjs` (driver at `local-e2e-proof-after-fix3/proof.mjs`). The driver (1) starts the fake provider and isolated gateway from a clean state dir; (2) opens a WebChat WebSocket and completes the `connect` handshake; (3) `chat.send` turn 1 prompting the provider to stream `MIXED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser code {"action":"status"}\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {"action":"status"}\nMIXED-AFTER`; (4) `agent.wait` until the run terminates, then `chat.history`; (5) `chat.send` turn 2 prompting the provider to stream `DOTTED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {"action":"status"}\nDOTTED-AFTER`; (6) `agent.wait` + `chat.history`; (7) scans captured `chat.history` assistant texts, live `agent` stream=assistant events, and `chat` assistant events for the leak needles (`commentary to=functions.browser`, `functions.browser.commentary`, `functions.browser code`, `functions.browser`).
- **Evidence after fix:** `local-e2e-proof-after-fix3/result.json` (verdict=PASS, all four leak needle scans clean), `local-e2e-proof-after-fix3/proof.mjs` (reproducible driver), `local-e2e-proof-after-fix3/gateway.log`, `local-e2e-proof-after-fix3/provider.log` (runtime logs from the isolated run).

```text
# fake provider streams back raw scaffolding
content = "MIXED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser code {\"action\":\"status\"}\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {\"action\":\"status\"}\nMIXED-AFTER"
# WebChat client drives a real turn
ws.connect -> chat.send(sessionKey="main", message=...) -> agent.wait -> chat.history
# assertions on the captured RPC + stream events
historyLeaked=false   # chat.history assistant text has no leak needles
streamLeaked=false    # live agent/chat deltas have no leak needles
mixedVisibleOk=true   # surrounding visible text preserved
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `providerSentRawNeedles=true` confirms the fake provider actually returned text containing `commentary to=functions.browser` and `functions.browser.commentary`, so the leak trigger condition is real. `historyLeaked=false`: `chat.history` assistant text blocks contain none of the leak needles; visible text collapses to `MIXED-BEFORE\nMIXED-AFTER` and `DOTTED-BEFORE\nDOTTED-AFTER`. `streamLeaked=false`: live `agent` (stream=assistant) and `chat` assistant events emit sanitized text only — no raw scaffolding reaches a streaming WebChat consumer. `mixedVisibleOk=true`, `dottedVisibleOk=true`: both scaffolding shapes (top-level `functions.browser` and namespaced `functions.browser.commentary`) are stripped while surrounding user-visible text is preserved. `agent.wait` for both turns returns `status: ok`, so the run genuinely executed the assistant path that triggers the sanitizer.
- **What was not tested:** Live proof against a real model account or a real messaging transport (Telegram, Discord, Slack, iMessage, WhatsApp, Desktop, Mantis) was not executed; the fake OpenAI-compatible provider stands in for the model. The leak path is fully reproducible without a real model, so this disclosure is about transport/account coverage, not about whether the fix holds. The Harmony plain-text tool-call parser and the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` implementation are unchanged in this commit; they were already introduced earlier in this PR and are reused as-is.

## Maintainer-ready checklist

- **Fix classification:** root-cause fix — closes two assistant-visible-text surfaces (chat history projection, embedded agent subscribe non-phase streaming) that bypassed the canonical sanitizer, by routing both through the same sanitizer instead of adding a one-off filter at either surface.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** high — the leak is reproducible end-to-end against an isolated gateway with a fake provider that emits raw scaffolding, and the post-fix run shows all four leak needle scans clean on both surfaces with surrounding visible text preserved.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** any WebChat user viewing an assistant reply that happened to contain a Harmony plain-text browser tool-call fragment would see internal scaffolding like `commentary to=functions.browser code {"action":"status"}` in the persisted history and during live streaming. This is the exact symptom in issue #89668; closing both surfaces restores the contract that assistant visible text is always sanitized.
- **What did NOT change:** the Harmony plain-text tool-call parser (`packages/tool-call-repair/`), the canonical sanitizer implementation in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`, any public API/schema/protocol/wire format/default value/migration/compatibility surface, and all non-WebChat delivery paths (Telegram, Discord, Slack, iMessage, WhatsApp, Desktop, Mantis) are out of scope and unchanged. The scope only adds sanitizer calls at two surfaces that previously bypassed it.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` is a projection/mirror path that derives WebChat display data from session state — it does not feed back into the model-consumed transcript, so routing its assistant text through the canonical sanitizer is a presentation-layer fix, not a change to the source-of-truth contract. The streaming handler change likewise only affects what is broadcast to WebChat clients, not what is persisted to the model-consumed transcript. The canonical sanitizer remains the single source of truth for what assistant text is allowed to be visible.
- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts` cover the two surfaces respectively.
- **Scenario locked in:** assistant history text block containing mixed top-level `commentary to=functions.browser` and namespaced `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary` scaffolding surrounded by visible text must collapse to just the surrounding visible text in `chat.history`, and live streaming `agent`/`chat` deltas must emit only the sanitized text.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the regression tests pin the exact leak payload from issue #89668 (both top-level and namespaced forms) at each surface and assert both leak needles are gone while surrounding visible text survives, so a regression that re-introduces either scaffolding form at either surface fails the test immediately.

## Patch quality notes

- **Patch quality notes:** the patch-quality scanner reports `default_return_added` warnings on `packages/tool-call-repair/src/stream-normalizer.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts` (e.g., `return false;`, `return true;` lines added). These are early-return sentinels in the namespaced-tool-call promotion logic introduced earlier in this PR (commit `8d6efe75`), not in the files this commit changes; they short-circuit promotion when the candidate target name fails a structural check (e.g., `functions.browser` must contain a dot and match an allowlist entry), which is the intended contract — without the early return, malformed names would fall through to the legacy path and re-introduce the leak. They are not silent fallbacks masking missing behavior; they are explicit structural validators. This commit's own changes in `chat-display-projection.ts` and `embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts` add no new default returns; they route existing text through an existing sanitizer, so no new patch-quality smell is introduced.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability, message-delivery, session-state (the three categories flagged by the patch-quality gate because this PR's files touch chat projection, agent subscribe handlers, and tool-call normalization).
- **Risk explanation:** *availability* — the sanitizer runs on the gateway hot path for every assistant text block in `chat.history` and on every non-phase streaming delta; the sanitizer is linear in text length and already runs on the persisted-history write path, so the incremental cost is one linear scan per surface with no quadratic behavior, and the worst case (very long assistant text) is bounded by the existing chat-history text truncation. *message-delivery* — the streaming change alters what text is broadcast on the `agent`/`chat` events; the alteration only removes scaffolding that should never have been visible, and the regression test pins the exact post-fix delta, so a delivery regression would fail the test. *session-state* — the change does not touch persisted session transcript writes (only the projection derived from them and the streaming broadcast), so model-consumed context is unaffected; the session transcript contract is unchanged.
- **Why acceptable:** the change only narrows what is shown to WebChat users and inherits its behavior from an already-shipped canonical sanitizer; both surfaces have pinned regression tests, and the local gateway E2E proof confirms both leak paths are closed with surrounding visible text preserved. No new public API, schema, protocol, or compatibility surface is introduced.
